perturbation.original_text: "Term" has the meaning set forth in Section 6.1.
perturbation.changed_text: "Term" has the meaning set forth in Section 6.1, and will be automatically extended indefinitely unless either party provides a written notice of termination at least 30 days prior to the end of the current term.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds an automatic renewal clause to the definition of 'Term,' which contradicts the original intent of having a defined Initial Term and a process for negotiating extensions as described in Section 6.1. The contradiction arises because the automatic renewal clause effectively eliminates the need for negotiation, creating uncertainty about the actual duration of the agreement and conflicts with section 6.1 that mentions a initial term and a negotiation of extension of the term
perturbation.contradicted_text: The initial term of this Agreement (the "Initial Term") shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue for a period of ten (10) years thereafter. Beginning twelve (12) months prior to the expiration of the Initial Term, the Parties shall use good faith efforts to negotiate an extension of the term of this Agreement (the Initial Term together with any applicable extension, the "Term").
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an automatic renewal clause into the definition of 'Term' in Section 6.1, while the contradicted text explicitly describes an initial term of ten years followed by a good faith negotiation for any extension. This creates a direct contradiction because the automatic renewal clause bypasses the negotiated extension process detailed in the contradicted section, making the original process and the need for negotiation uncertain and creating conflicting obligations. The changed text, in effect, overrides the original intent of having a defined term subject to negotiation, which creates uncertainty about the agreement's actual duration and enforceability of the negotiation clause.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall ensure that the Business complies with all applicable Laws in respect of operation, advertising and promotion of the Business and use of the Licensed Domain Names and Licensed Content in connection therewith.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall ensure that the Business complies with all applicable Laws in respect of operation, advertising and promotion of the Business and use of the Licensed Domain Names and Licensed Content in connection therewith. However, Licensor will provide legal counsel to guarantee the compliance of the business with all applicable laws and will be responsible for any issues arising from the non-compliance of laws.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that while the Licensee is responsible for compliance with laws, the Licensor will be responsible for legal counsel and any issues arising from the non-compliance of laws. This contradicts the Licensee's explicit responsibility and creates uncertainty about who is ultimately liable for compliance issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee shall ensure that the Business complies with all applicable Laws in respect of operation, advertising and promotion of the Business and use of the Licensed Domain Names and Licensed Content in connection therewith. However, Licensor will provide legal counsel to guarantee the compliance of the business with all applicable laws and will be responsible for any issues arising from the non-compliance of laws.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by assigning responsibility for legal compliance and potential liabilities arising from non-compliance to both the Licensee and the Licensor. Originally, the Licensee had sole responsibility, but the modification shifts some of that responsibility to the Licensor, creating a conflict and uncertainty about ultimate liability. This directly contradicts the initial clause that states the Licensee is responsible for ensuring compliance. Since the changed text and contradicted text are the same, the original responsibility assigned has been fundamentally altered.
